name: HBV_DR1_DR2_origin
description: HBV direct repeat regions DR1 (nt 1824) and DR2 (nt 1590), each an 11-nt motif (5'-TTCACCTCTGC-3') that serve as priming sites for plus-strand DNA synthesis. The terminal redundancy r (~9 nt) at the ends of the minus-strand DNA pairs DR1 to DR2 to circularize the genome during reverse transcription. Critical for replication and host integration events: virtually all chromosomal integration junctions cluster near DR1, making this the principal integration-hotspot motif. The DR1/DR2/r triad defines the cohesive overlap of the partially dsDNA rcDNA.
expected_copy_count: 2 direct repeats per genome plus the terminal redundancy r
